title: Marketing-sourced deals close 27% faster this week
anchor: Current week wins average 38 days versus the 11-week baseline of 52 days.
connect: Mid-market represents 7 of 9 wins in the current period.
body: Current week wins average 38 days versus the 11-week baseline of 52 days. Mid-market represents 7 of 9 wins in the current period.
trace: Significant velocity improvement from marketing-sourced deals this week compared to prior 11-week baseline.

title: Branded search climbs six weeks running
anchor: Weekly volumes moved from 1000 to 1921, a 92% gain over the six-week window.
connect: Direct traffic crossed organic search on April 15th for the first time.
body: Weekly volumes moved from 1000 to 1921, a 92% gain over the six-week window. Direct traffic crossed organic search on April 15th for the first time.
trace: Six consecutive weeks of branded search growth coinciding with direct traffic overtaking organic as primary channel.

title: Paid social outperformed paid search this quarter
anchor: Social generated $620K in pipeline versus search's $380K in Q2.
connect: Q1 ran the opposite direction with search at $720K and social at $410K.
body: Social generated $620K in pipeline versus search's $380K in Q2. Q1 ran the opposite direction with search at $720K and social at $410K.
trace: Complete reversal in paid channel performance between Q1 and Q2, with social now leading.

title: Enterprise win rate jumped 8 points in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise wins at 31% compared to the four-quarter trailing average of 22%.
connect: Average deal size increased from $142K to $187K over the same comparison.
body: Q2 enterprise wins at 31% compared to the four-quarter trailing average of 22%. Average deal size increased from $142K to $187K over the same comparison.
trace: Both win rate and ASP improvements in enterprise segment during Q2.

title: April mid-market SQLs up 50% from quarterly baseline
anchor: Month-to-date volume at 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: ABM acceptance rate runs at 61% compared to 42% for non-ABM leads.
body: Month-to-date volume at 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. ABM acceptance rate runs at 61% compared to 42% for non-ABM leads.
trace: Strong SQL volume increase in April with ABM showing significantly higher acceptance rates.

title: Event pipeline velocity compressed by 35%
anchor: Q1 event-sourced wins closed in 46 days versus Q4's 72-day average.
connect: SaaS Connect contributed 12 of the 17 total event wins in Q1.
body: Q1 event-sourced wins closed in 46 days versus Q4's 72-day average. SaaS Connect contributed 12 of the 17 total event wins in Q1.
trace: Significant improvement in event-sourced deal velocity with SaaS Connect as primary contributor.

title: Atlas mentions lead the competitive set by 52%
anchor: April brand mentions at 1840 versus Beacon Systems at 1210.
connect: Analyst mentions doubled over the last two weeks with 9 versus the prior 30-day count of 6.
body: April brand mentions at 1840 versus Beacon Systems at 1210. Analyst mentions doubled over the last two weeks with 9 versus the prior 30-day count of 6.
trace: Strong brand mention performance versus primary competitor with increasing analyst attention.

title: Digital ads budget carries $84K of headroom
anchor: Q2 actual spend at $256K versus the $340K plan through April 23rd.
connect: The dollars reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
body: Q2 actual spend at $256K versus the $340K plan through April 23rd. The dollars reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
trace: Under-spend in digital ads being redirected to event marketing initiatives.

title: SDR capacity absorbed full inbound load this week
anchor: Team capacity at 210 versus actual inbound volume of 142.
connect: The four-week trailing average ran capacity at 206 with volume at 201.
body: Team capacity at 210 versus actual inbound volume of 142. The four-week trailing average ran capacity at 206 with volume at 201.
trace: SDR team has current capacity cushion after weeks of running near full utilization.

title: Fintech dominates mid-market closed wins
anchor: 8 of 12 recent mid-market wins came from fintech vertical.
connect: All 12 winners run Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
body: 8 of 12 recent mid-market wins came from fintech vertical. All 12 winners run Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
trace: Strong vertical and technology stack patterns in recent mid-market wins.

title: Atlas Assist launch locks calendar this Friday
anchor: Product ships May 8th with campaign assets ready.
connect: Calendar coordination locks April 24th across all channels.
body: Product ships May 8th with campaign assets ready. Calendar coordination locks April 24th across all channels.
trace: Upcoming product launch with campaign coordination finalizing this week.

title: Reference opt-ins vary by 46 points across products
anchor: Atlas Insights leads at 75% with Atlas Connect trailing at 29%.
connect: Atlas Workflow sits in the middle at 70% opt-in rate.
body: Atlas Insights leads at 75% with Atlas Connect trailing at 29%. Atlas Workflow sits in the middle at 70% opt-in rate.
trace: Wide variation in customer willingness to serve as references across product lines.

title: Target accounts show concentrated high-intent activity
anchor: 3 accounts logged 5+ high-intent events in the current week.
connect: The activity spans March ABM additions and core named account lists from a 50-account universe.
body: 3 accounts logged 5+ high-intent events in the current week. The activity spans March ABM additions and core named account lists from a 50-account universe.
trace: High engagement concentration among target accounts with activity from both newer and established lists.

title: Forrester and G2 drive analyst mention cluster
anchor: Both firms contributed 3 mentions each over the last 14 days.
connect: Gartner added 1 mention with 2 additional firms completing the total.
body: Both firms contributed 3 mentions each over the last 14 days. Gartner added 1 mention with 2 additional firms completing the total.
trace: Analyst attention concentrated in specific firms with Forrester and G2 leading coverage.

title: Direct traffic emerged as primary channel mid-April
anchor: Direct reached 42% share versus organic search at 37% through April.
connect: The crossover happened April 15th after months of organic leading the mix.
body: Direct reached 42% share versus organic search at 37% through April. The crossover happened April 15th after months of organic leading the mix.
trace: Significant shift in traffic composition with direct overtaking organic search as primary source.
